desc: Score, color, and timing updates run only when an event is received, so they freeze whenever the event queue is empty.
The score calculation uses int(delta * 10) % 7, discarding fractional elapsed time and wrapping larger intervals into nonmonotonic score increments.
Using time.time() for elapsed-time measurement allows system-clock adjustments to produce negative or otherwise incorrect deltas.
Key presses can reduce velocity to zero or below, causing left and down controls to reverse direction while right and up continue using its magnitude.
Player displacement is applied per rendered frame instead of scaling by elapsed time, so movement speed changes when the achieved frame rate changes.
draw_stuff constructs a new system Font on every frame, causing unnecessary repeated resource lookup and allocation in the hot render loop.
Initialization and the blocking game loop execute at module import time, preventing safe reuse or import by tests and other modules.
